title: per-bridge configuration table — central TSV replaces scattered env exports
summary: Introduce state/bridge_config.tsv as a single source of truth for per-bridge TTL, min_interval, UA, and accept-header. bridge_helper.sh now resolves these fields with precedence chain: CLI arg > env var > config-file row > compiled default. Backward-compat: per-bridge .hexa callers unchanged; explicit args still win. Migration to simplified .hexa calls deferred to next cycle (axis hygiene: hexa-only sessions, helper is .sh).

predecessors.omega_bridge_2_3: 16 bridges migrated to bridge_helper.sh with hardcoded TTL + min_interval per call
predecessors.omega_bridge_4: 8 bridges set BRIDGE_HELPER_UA env per call (cmb/nanograv/icecube/arxiv/openalex/lhc/uniprot/wikipedia)
predecessors.omega_bridge_11: BRIDGE_HELPER_AUTH support (mode-0600 tempfile, no argv leak, sha1[0:8] cache-key suffix)

context.problem: Per-bridge config (TTL/UA/headers) is scattered across 16 .hexa files. Each policy change requires N edits. Audit surface is N × 16 files. Single config table is more maintainable + auditable + diffable.

additions: Header docblock for ω-bridge-12 documenting precedence chain
_load_bridge_config(bridge) helper — awk-extracts TAB-separated row, sets CFG_TTL/CFG_MIN_INT/CFG_UA/CFG_ACCEPT, treats '-' as unset, silent no-op on miss
cmd_fetch ttl/min_interval resolution rewritten to use precedence chain with verbose source-tagged logs
extra_args UA + accept-header resolution extended to fall back to CFG_UA / CFG_ACCEPT when env vars unset
behavioural_invariants: When config file absent → identical behaviour to ω-bridge-11
When bridge row missing → identical behaviour to ω-bridge-11 (compiled defaults)
When CLI arg present → CLI value wins (existing 16 .hexa callers unchanged)
When BRIDGE_HELPER_UA env present → env wins over config UA
When BRIDGE_HELPER_HEADERS env present → env wins over config accept (entire env-headers block replaces config-derived accept header)

config_file.format: TAB-separated; header comments prefixed '#'; one row per bridge

ttl_rationale.1800: arxiv / openalex / wikipedia — semi-fast scholarly + encyclopedic data (30 min)
ttl_rationale.3600: codata / gw / icecube / pubchem / simbad / uniprot — medium-velocity (1 h)
ttl_rationale.86400: cmb / gaia / lhc / nanograv / nist_atomic / oeis — canonical / slow-changing (24 h)

config_file.min_interval_rationale: 1s default for polite spacing; 5s for horizons + icecube (heavier per-call cost / NASA + experiment endpoints)

precedence_chain.rationale: CLI > env > config > default mirrors UNIX convention. Today's hardcoded .hexa calls (CLI args + env vars) keep working unchanged; config table provides defaults for any caller that omits them. Future cycle simplifies .hexa calls to omit hardcoded TTL/UA — config covers them.

tests.name: no CLI args → config TTL used
tests.cmd: BRIDGE_HELPER_VERBOSE=1 bash bridge_helper.sh fetch codata file:///tmp/bh12_test.json
tests.expected_log: ttl source=CONFIG val=3600 / min_interval source=CONFIG val=1
tests.result: PASS — observed exactly: 'ttl source=CONFIG val=3600' and 'min_interval source=CONFIG val=1'

tests.name: explicit CLI args → CLI wins
tests.cmd: BRIDGE_HELPER_VERBOSE=1 bash bridge_helper.sh fetch codata file:///tmp/bh12_test.json 999 7
tests.expected_log: ttl source=CLI val=999 / min_interval source=CLI val=7
tests.result: PASS — observed exactly: 'ttl source=CLI val=999' and 'min_interval source=CLI val=7'

tests.name: uniprot UA + accept resolved from config (no env)
tests.cmd: BRIDGE_HELPER_VERBOSE=1 bash bridge_helper.sh fetch uniprot file:///tmp/bh12_test.json
tests.expected_log: UA set (source=CONFIG): nexus-uniprot-bridge/... + header set (source=CONFIG): accept: application/json

tests.name: env BRIDGE_HELPER_UA overrides config UA
tests.cmd: BRIDGE_HELPER_UA=env-override-ua/1.0 bash bridge_helper.sh fetch uniprot file:///tmp/bh12_test.json
tests.result: PASS — env wins; config accept still emitted (orthogonal)

tests.name: unknown bridge → compiled defaults (no config row)
tests.expected_log: ttl source=DEFAULT val=3600 / min_interval source=DEFAULT val=1
tests.result: PASS — defaults correctly engaged on missing row

tests.name: spot-check uniprot cache-hit through config-driven path
tests.result: PASS — observed 'CACHE HIT bridge=uniprot age=3598s ttl=3600s' (TTL=3600 came from config table)

backward_compat.compat_argument: All 16 .hexa callers pass explicit positional ttl/min_interval (and 8 set BRIDGE_HELPER_UA env, 2 set BRIDGE_HELPER_HEADERS env). Per the precedence chain, CLI + env always win over config — so existing behaviour is bit-identical. Config table only takes effect when a caller omits these; today none do, so it acts as documentation + future-default. This is opt-in rollout: the config table is wired in but inert for current traffic. Next cycle simplifies .hexa calls to opt in.
backward_compat.regression_surface: Zero. The _load_bridge_config call is the only new code path executed on every fetch; on missing config file it returns immediately (single -f test).

next_cycle_omega_bridge_13: Simplify per-bridge .hexa fetch invocations: drop hardcoded TTL/min_interval positional args (config now provides them).
Drop BRIDGE_HELPER_UA env exports from 8 bridges (config provides UA).
Drop BRIDGE_HELPER_HEADERS env exports from 2 bridges (uniprot, wikipedia — config accept covers them).
Per-bridge change reduces .hexa cmd assembly from a 100-char line to a ~50-char line.
Audit benefit: any future TTL or UA change is a 1-line TSV edit instead of 16 .hexa edits.
config_evolution_options: Add row-level enable/disable flag (skip in bridge_health sweep)
Promote to JSON if schema grows past ~6 columns (TSV remains best at this size)

compliance.hexa_only_session: RESPECTED — only state/*.tsv (data) and tool/bridge_helper.sh (tracked .sh, sweeper-safe per design); zero .hexa edits this cycle
compliance.no_live_api_hammering: RESPECTED — validation T1-T5 used file:// URLs; T6 used cached uniprot response (age=3598s, no live call)
compliance.raw_66: config-load failures degrade silently to compiled defaults (sensible) but are surfaced via verbose log with explicit source tags
compliance.raw_71: verbose diagnostics on stderr; stdout body unchanged
compliance.raw_77: config TSV is human-diffable, append-friendly (new bridges = append a row)
